Warsh’s congressional debut is about to take place, and Wall Street is shouting: You don’t have to give an interest rate path, but please make your views on the economy clear.

Since taking office, Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh has been extremely cautious to avoid signaling the direction of interest rates, which has obscured another piece of information that is crucial to investors, analysts and even other policymakers - how he will respond to economic challenges.

Warsh will appear before Congress on Tuesday and Wednesday, where Fed watchers will be closely watching his views on inflation, the labor market and economic growth, and how those factors interact with interest rates. But if his recent public appearances are any guide, these observers may still find little to gain.

Warsh has pledged to jettison the central bank's so-called "forward guidance" - signals about the path of interest rates. At his first press conference on June 17, he avoided answering many related questions.

For example, when asked how patient policymakers would be in waiting for inflation to come down, he responded: "Your question sounds like you're encouraging me to give forward guidance." This has continued to build dissatisfaction outside the Fed, and one of Warsh's influential colleagues has spoken out publicly about it.

In a speech in Rome last week, Fed Governor Christopher Waller made a point of distinguishing between "providing forward guidance" and "illustrating how the central bank might respond under different economic scenarios." He noted that the latter would help reduce uncertainty faced by markets and households, "making life easier for everyone." Goldman Sachs:

The inflationary impact of the Middle East conflict is weakening, and the Federal Reserve is expected to keep interest rates unchanged during the year Goldman Sachs said that the United States and Iran have recently launched attacks on each other again, and shipping risks in the Strait of Hormuz have increased, but the impact on commodity prices caused by the war has significantly receded.

Oil prices have only rebounded modestly and are still about 30% lower than their highs from the end of April to early May; U.S. gasoline prices fell by 15%, aviation fuel prices fell by 35%, and most Gulf export commodities such as methanol, polyethylene and nitrogen fertilizer are close to pre-war levels.

Although sea and air transportation costs continue to rise, international transportation only accounts for 1% to 2% of the import cost of consumer goods in the United States, and its impact on terminal prices is limited.

Although Gulf crude oil flows have declined, they are still above wartime lows, and global visible oil inventories are not at unusually low levels. The Goldman Sachs model shows that the incremental impact of the conflict on core PCE quarter-on-quarter inflation has peaked in the second quarter, especially from May to June.

If the situation does not escalate, the related pressure will weaken significantly in the third and fourth quarters. Supply shortages and supply chain pressures are far lower than during the epidemic, and current inflation expectations have not been significantly anchored.

The marginal impact of superimposed tariffs and AI demand on monthly inflation has subsided. Goldman Sachs expects core PCE to increase by 0.24% month-on-month in June and then maintain at 0.20% to 0.23%; the year-on-year growth rate in August will fall by 0.2 percentage points to 3.2% due to adjustments to statistical methods.

The bank expects the Federal Reserve to keep interest rates unchanged for the rest of 2026, but the policy room for error is limited.

If the conflict escalates again and pushes oil prices back to US$100 per barrel, core inflation may rise an additional 3 to 4 basis points month-on-month in the coming months and significantly increase the risk of interest rate hikes.

Bank of America: Global cloud capital expenditure will reach US$1.15 trillion in 2027, and the AI investment cycle remains sustainable The latest research report from Bank of America Securities predicts that the capital expenditures of the world's largest cloud vendors will reach US$197 billion in the second quarter, a year-on-year increase of 30% and a

year-on-year increase of 83%; capital expenditures in 2026 and 2027 will increase to US$851 billion and US$1.15 trillion respectively, a year-on-year increase of 78% and 35%, significantly higher than the May forecast. Bank of America believes that investment in AI infrastructure is sustainable.

The remaining performance obligations and order backlog disclosed by Microsoft, Amazon, Google and Oracle total more than $2 trillion, providing contract demand support for construction from 2026 to 2028.

At the same time, AI commercialization is accelerating: OpenAI's revenue is expected to increase from US$13 billion in 2025 to US$62 billion in 2027, and Anthropic's annual revenue has exceeded US$47 billion as of May 2026; the number of tokens generated by the agent workload can reach 10 to 1,000 times that of ordinary conversations, further increasing the demand for computing power.

High investment will still squeeze cash flow. Bank of America predicts that cloud vendor capital expenditures will reach approximately 100% to 115% of operating cash flow from 2026 to 2028, and industry free cash flow profit margins will drop to -1%, -5%, and -4% respectively, far lower than the historical 15% to 20%.

However, related expenses can basically be covered by operating cash flow. Since 2026, the five major cloud vendors have raised approximately US$244 billion, mainly long-term debt and permanent equity from 3 to 40 years. Bank of America believes that this is a balance sheet optimization and not a signal of financial pressure.

The bank is optimistic about Nvidia, Broadcom, Marvell, AMD, Micron and the semiconductor equipment industry chain.

SpaceX Starship conducts its 13th test flight on Thursday and 20 V3 satellites will be deployed for the first time SpaceX previewed the 13th comprehensive flight test of its Starship system on the X platform. The earliest launch time is 6:45 pm Eastern Time on Thursday, with a launch window of 90 minutes.

This mission comes approximately seven weeks after the 12th flight on May 22. This test flight will use the latest Starship equipped with a Raptor 3 engine and a super-heavy V3 launch vehicle. The booster will attempt to ignite and return, then splash down in the Gulf of Mexico, while Starship No. 40 will fly along a suborbital path.

The Starlink V3 satellite is equipped with advanced laser links for inter-satellite communications, deployable solar arrays and onboard cameras, six of which will capture images of the starship's heat shield during flight. If the 13th mission is successful, SpaceX may be able to conduct the first orbital launch of the starship on the next flight.

SpaceX is working hard to advance the service of Starship to deploy Starlink V3 satellites and serve as a lunar lander for NASA's Artemis lunar landing program. U.S. Stock Futures Fall as Renewed U.S.-Iran Fighting Sends Oil Sharply Higher

U.S. equity futures opened lower Monday as fresh strikes between the U.S. and Iran raised concerns about potential disruption to oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

S&P 500 futures ($ES) fell 0.3 percent while Nasdaq 100 futures ($NQ) dropped 1 percent. Dow Jones Industrial Average futures ($YM) were roughly flat in early trading.

Oil prices surged on the escalation. Brent crude ($BRN) rose more than 3.5 percent to around $78.80 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate crude ($CL) gained a similar amount to near $74 a barrel. The moves mark some of the largest daily gains in weeks as traders priced in risks to energy supplies.

The latest round of fighting comes after a weekend of renewed exchanges, including reports of attacks on vessels and strikes on energy-related sites. Analysts noted that any sustained disruption to traffic through the Strait of Hormuz could tighten global oil markets further.

Chip Stocks Under Pressure

Semiconductor shares were among the weakest areas. SK Hynix Inc. shares fell sharply in Seoul, down about 15 percent, after the company’s strong Nasdaq debut last week. The pullback weighed on broader chip sentiment, with European semiconductor names also trading lower.

The moves come amid ongoing questions about the durability of AI-related spending by hyperscalers, even as the sector has shown resilience in recent sessions.

Other Markets

Gold futures ($GC) slid more than 1 percent as rising oil prices and higher Treasury yields reduced safe-haven demand. The 10-year Treasury yield edged higher, while the U.S. dollar was little changed. Bitcoin fell around 1.6 percent, reflecting the broader risk-off tone.

What to Watch

This week is expected to be eventful. Investors will focus on U.S. inflation data, including consumer and producer price reports, for clues on how higher energy prices may affect the inflation outlook. Several major banks, including JPMorgan Chase & Co. ($JPM) and Goldman Sachs Group Inc. ($GS), are scheduled to report earnings.

Markets will also continue monitoring developments in the Middle East and whether the latest escalation leads to further volatility in energy and risk assets.

MARKET UPDATE — MONDAY, 13 JULY 2026

Markets are beginning the week with two major threats arriving simultaneously:

🔸 renewed US–Iran fighting is pushing oil and bond yields higher;

🔸 a violent semiconductor sell-off is testing the crowded AI trade.

The Nasdaq is under pressure, volatility is rising and Asian markets have fallen sharply.

But this is not yet broad market panic.

It is a concentrated risk-off move centred on technology, energy inflation and geopolitical uncertainty.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 US–IRAN FIGHTING PUTS HORMUZ BACK AT THE CENTRE

The US and Iran exchanged five major waves of attacks over the weekend and into Monday morning, as their struggle for control of the Strait of Hormuz intensified.

The US said it targeted Iranian:

🔸 air-defence systems;

🔸 coastal radar sites;

🔸 missile and drone capabilities;

🔸 military boats operating around the strait.

Iran responded by claiming attacks on US-linked military facilities in Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman and Jordan.

Tehran says the Strait of Hormuz is closed.

Washington disputes that claim and says escorted vessels are still passing.

The most accurate conclusion is that the strait is severely disrupted, but not completely sealed.

Commercial traffic has slowed dramatically as shipping companies avoid exposing vessels and crews to missiles, drones and attacks at sea.

Simple explanation:

The Strait of Hormuz is like a narrow doorway used by a huge part of the world’s energy supply.

The doorway does not need to be physically locked to cause a crisis.

If ships consider it too dangerous to pass, the practical result can be almost the same.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 OIL RISES AS ENERGY-SUPPLY FEARS RETURN

Brent crude climbed approximately 4% towards $79 a barrel, while US WTI crude rose towards $74.

Oil remains below the extreme levels reached earlier in the war, but the renewed increase matters because markets had started expecting shipping conditions to improve.

Those expectations are now being reversed.

Before the conflict, approximately one-fifth of global oil supplies passed through Hormuz.

That makes disruption around the strait a direct threat to global energy availability, inflation and economic growth.

No LNG carriers were reported to have transited Hormuz since Saturday, while European natural-gas prices also rose.

Europe is particularly exposed because its jet-fuel stocks provide less than one month of demand cover.

Britain, France and Germany could face particular pressure if Middle Eastern supplies are interrupted for a prolonged period.

Simple explanation:

Higher oil does not remain confined to petrol stations.

It raises the cost of flying, transporting goods, manufacturing products and delivering food.

Eventually, those costs can reach consumers through higher prices.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 MARKETS TURN CAUTIOUS—BUT THIS IS NOT PANIC

The latest US futures figures show:

🔸 S&P 500 futures: 7,601.50, −0.25%

🔸 Nasdaq 100 futures: 29,790, −0.81%

🔸 Dow futures: 52,885, −0.04%

🔸 VIX: 16.30, +8.45%

Technology shares are taking the greatest pressure, while the Dow is almost unchanged.

That tells us investors are not indiscriminately selling everything.

They are reducing exposure primarily to expensive AI and semiconductor shares.

The VIX—often called Wall Street’s fear gauge—has risen more than 8%.

However, its absolute level remains close to 16.

Simple explanation:

Fear has increased, but it has not reached panic levels.

This is currently a selective risk-off move, rather than a broad market collapse.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 THE SELL-OFF SPREADS ACROSS ASIA

The weakness is not confined to US technology futures.

South Korea’s KOSPI fell almost 9%, triggering a temporary halt in trading.

Japan’s Nikkei 225 declined approximately 2%, while China’s CSI 300 fell roughly 1.7%.

The broader Asian retreat shows that investors are reacting to several connected risks:

🔸 rising oil prices;

🔸 worsening geopolitical tension;

🔸 higher borrowing costs;

🔸 profit-taking after extraordinary AI-related gains.

This is important because it shows the shock is global, even though semiconductor shares are at its centre.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 SK HYNIX SUFFERS A VIOLENT REVERSAL

SK Hynix fell more than 15% in Seoul—its steepest one-day decline in nearly two decades—immediately after its blockbuster US listing.

Samsung Electronics also fell approximately 11%.

On Friday, SK Hynix’s American depositary receipts surged more than 12% during their Nasdaq debut after the company raised approximately $26.5bn.

But the shares had already produced extraordinary gains before the listing.

SK Hynix had risen several-fold over the previous year as investors chased exposure to high-bandwidth memory chips used in AI data centres.

South Korean investors appear to have used the successful US debut as an opportunity to take profits.

Its US-listed ADRs were approximately 9% lower before Monday’s opening bell.

The pressure spread across global semiconductor shares:

🔸 Micron fell approximately 5% premarket;

🔸 Intel and Marvell declined approximately 3%;

🔸 Nvidia, AMD and Applied Materials also moved lower;

🔸 ASML, Infineon and other European semiconductor shares declined.

Simple explanation:

When almost everyone already owns the same popular trade, fewer new buyers remain.

That means even good news can trigger selling because investors decide the price has moved too far ahead of reality.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 THE AI TRADE IS BEING REPRICED—NOT DISPROVED

The semiconductor sell-off does not mean that demand for AI infrastructure has disappeared.

TSMC reported record second-quarter revenue of approximately T$1.27tn—or $39.6bn—representing growth of 36% from a year earlier.

The figure slightly exceeded market expectations.

June revenue alone rose almost 68% year on year.

TSMC manufactures advanced processors for companies including Nvidia and Apple, making its performance one of the strongest indicators of real semiconductor demand.

The company will report full second-quarter earnings and provide its latest outlook on Thursday.

That guidance could become one of the week’s most important tests for the entire AI trade.

Simple explanation:

Two things can be true simultaneously:

🔸 the AI industry can continue growing rapidly;

🔸 AI-related share prices can still fall because investors previously paid too much.

Strong businesses do not always equal attractive shares at every price.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 TREASURY YIELDS RISE ACROSS THE CURVE

US Treasury yields moved higher:

🔸 1-year yield: 4.073%, up 1.3 basis points

🔸 2-year yield: 4.221%, up 0.9 basis point

🔸 10-year yield: 4.575%, up 1.4 basis points

🔸 30-year yield: 5.077%, up 1.8 basis points

The 30-year yield remains above 5%, keeping pressure on expensive technology shares and other assets valued heavily on distant future profits.

Rising short-term yields suggest markets remain concerned about Federal Reserve policy.

Rising long-term yields suggest investors are also worried about persistent inflation, heavy government borrowing and the longer-term cost of capital.

Simple explanation:

When investors fear that inflation may remain high, they demand a greater return for lending money.

That pushes bond yields upwards.

Higher yields also make bonds more competitive with shares, particularly highly valued technology companies.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 GOLD FALLS DESPITE THE GEOPOLITICAL CRISIS

Gold fell more than 1% towards $4,066 an ounce.

That may appear surprising because gold is often considered a safe-haven asset.

However, Treasury yields are rising, while gold pays no interest.

The Dollar Index is actually slightly lower at approximately 100.88, meaning today’s pressure on gold appears to be coming more from rising yields than from dollar strength.

Simple explanation:

Investors may prefer interest-paying government bonds when yields rise, even during geopolitical uncertainty.

Gold can therefore fall despite increased fear.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 BITCOIN TRADES LIKE A RISK ASSET

Bitcoin fell approximately 2% towards $62,700–$62,900 as investors reduced exposure to volatile assets.

Bitcoin is sometimes described as digital gold.

But during sudden market shocks, it frequently behaves more like a highly volatile technology investment.

Simple explanation:

When investors urgently want safety or cash, they often sell the assets that move most dramatically first.

Today, Bitcoin is behaving more like a risk asset than a safe haven.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 FED SPEAKERS COME BEFORE TOMORROW’S MAJOR TEST

Fed governors Christopher Waller and Michelle Bowman are due to speak today.

Investors will be listening for clues about whether renewed oil inflation is changing their view of interest rates.

Waller’s comments may be particularly important.

Markets will want to know whether he still considers monetary policy sufficiently restrictive now that:

🔸 inflation has remained stubborn;

🔸 oil prices are rising again;

🔸 Treasury yields are moving higher;

🔸 economic growth remains resilient.

Tomorrow, Fed Chair Kevin Warsh will present the Federal Reserve’s semi-annual monetary-policy report to Congress.

His testimony comes on the same day as the June US consumer-price report.

That combination could produce major moves in bonds, currencies and technology shares.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 INFLATION DATA COULD DETERMINE THE NEXT MARKET MOVE

🔸Tuesday brings the June consumer-price index.

🔸Wednesday brings producer-price inflation.

Investors will be looking for evidence that higher energy costs are spreading into the wider economy.

Simple explanation:

Consumer inflation measures what households pay.

Producer inflation measures the costs faced by businesses.

If business costs rise, companies may eventually pass those increases on to customers.

A hotter-than-expected inflation report would increase the risk that the Federal Reserve keeps rates high—or raises them further.

A softer reading could calm bond markets, although another sustained oil-price surge would still complicate the outlook.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 BANK EARNINGS WILL TEST WALL STREET OPTIMISM

Earnings season accelerates tomorrow with results from:

🔸 JPMorgan Chase;

🔸 Goldman Sachs;

🔸 Bank of America;

🔸 Wells Fargo;

🔸 Citigroup.

The five largest US investment banks are expected to generate approximately $11.1bn in second-quarter investment-banking fees—around 27% more than a year earlier.

Equity-capital-market fees alone are forecast to reach approximately $2.5bn, potentially the strongest result since 2021.

IPOs, share sales and mergers have revived, generating valuable fees for Wall Street.

Simple explanation:

Investment banks make money when companies:

🔸 list shares;

🔸 raise capital;

🔸 buy other businesses;

🔸 sell divisions;

🔸 restructure debt.

More corporate deals mean more fees.

But expectations are already high.

Strong results may not be enough unless bank executives also provide confident guidance about lending, consumer health, trading activity and future dealmaking.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 CHINA’S ECONOMY FACES ANOTHER GROWTH TEST

China’s economy is expected to have grown approximately 4.5% from a year earlier during the second quarter, slowing from 5% in the first quarter.

Exports remain relatively resilient, partly helped by AI-related demand.

But domestic consumption, private investment and the property market remain weak.

China’s second-quarter GDP, retail-sales, industrial-production and investment data are due on Wednesday.

Simple explanation:

China’s factories are still selling substantial quantities of goods abroad.

But Chinese households and private companies remain cautious about spending at home.

That makes economic growth increasingly dependent on exports and government support.

Investors will be watching for signs that Beijing may introduce additional fiscal stimulus later this year.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 THE US LABOUR MARKET LOOKS STRONGER THAN IT FEELS

The unemployment rate remains low, but hiring has slowed sharply.

Economists increasingly describe the current environment as “low-hire, low-fire”:

🔸 companies are not dismissing many existing workers;

🔸 but they are also reluctant to create new positions;

🔸 unemployed people and graduates are struggling to get hired;

🔸 fewer workers are confident enough to leave their jobs.

Simple explanation:

The unemployment rate tells us that relatively few people are being fired.

It does not tell us that finding a new job is easy.

The labour market may be relatively safe for people already employed, but extremely difficult for those trying to enter it.

This matters because even employed consumers may spend less when they fear that replacing a lost job would be difficult.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🔸 June US consumer inflation

🔸 China trade data

🔸 Fed Chair Kevin Warsh testifies before the House Financial Services Committee

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 WHAT IT MEANS

Today’s market is sending seven important messages.

1️⃣ Hormuz remains the world’s most important immediate market risk

The crisis matters because it threatens one of the world’s most important energy arteries.

Even partial disruption can lift oil, natural gas, shipping and insurance costs.

2️⃣ Oil has become an inflation trade again

A sustained rise would reduce the Federal Reserve’s freedom to cut rates and could increase the risk of further tightening.

3️⃣ The AI trade is being repriced, not disproved

TSMC’s record revenue shows that demand remains powerful.

SK Hynix’s fall shows what can happen when strong fundamentals meet extreme valuations, leverage and crowded positioning.

4️⃣ The market is showing stress, not panic

The Nasdaq is falling much more than the Dow, while the VIX remains near 16.

Investors are reducing exposure selectively rather than selling every asset indiscriminately.

5️⃣ Long-term bond yields remain a major threat

The 30-year Treasury yield above 5% increases financing costs and places pressure on companies whose valuations depend heavily on future growth.

6️⃣ Inflation and earnings now matter even more

Oil has already revived inflation anxiety.

This week’s CPI, PPI, bank earnings and TSMC outlook will reveal whether economic and corporate fundamentals can withstand higher borrowing costs.

7️⃣ Global growth is becoming increasingly uneven

🔸 The US labour market has low layoffs but weak hiring.

🔸China has resilient exports but weak domestic demand.

🔸AI investment remains strong, while consumers and traditional industries face greater pressure.

This is not a simple strong or weak economy.

It is an increasingly divided one.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 BOTTOM LINE

The market entered Monday facing two very different challenges.

The US–Iran conflict threatens the flow of energy through the global economy.

The semiconductor sell-off challenges the valuations supporting global equity markets.

Oil is rising.

Treasury yields are rising.

The Nasdaq is falling.

Volatility is increasing.

Bitcoin is weakening.

Yet TSMC’s record revenue shows that the underlying AI-investment cycle remains strong.

This is not a straightforward collapse in fundamentals.

It is a market confronting excessive expectations, crowded positioning, geopolitical instability and renewed inflation risk—all at the same time.

The next major direction will probably be determined by four forces:

🔸 whether Hormuz traffic deteriorates or recovers;

🔸 whether inflation surprises higher;

🔸 what Kevin Warsh says about interest rates;

🔸 whether bank and semiconductor earnings justify current valuations.

Multiple institutions argue that while certain segments exhibit elevated valuations and overheated expectations, this AI cycle differs from the 2000 internet bubble in that it is underpinned by genuine profitability and industrial demand. Market opportunities are more structural than broad-based, and investors are advised to focus on profit realization and balanced portfolio construction.

Citic Securities (https://t.co/fLN5a98rLU) released a report stating that after the cooling of speculative trading, attention should shift to the recovery of non-AI sectors. For AI-related segments, preference is given to stocks with strong supply constraints, low valuations, and mid-to-downstream exposure, such as cloud providers, memory, servers, gas turbines, and diesel generator sets. For non-AI segments, select basic metals (copper, tin), new energy (awaiting negative sentiment in the supply chain to dissipate and a return to fundamentals), and chemicals (refrigerants, phosphorus chemicals, polyurethane, dyes, large-scale petrochemicals). The firm continues to favor innovative pharmaceuticals with strong overseas expansion and low-valued securities firms.

Securities Times launched the “Times Economic Eye: Second Quarter 2026 Economist Survey.” As of July 10, 61 responses were collected. Respondents generally maintained a positive outlook on A-share performance, expressing optimism regarding market vitality and the long-term prospects of the AI sector. Regarding whether the AI sector is overvalued, nearly 70% of respondents believe a short-term correction is possible, but the long-term outlook remains favorable.

Net inflows via the Stock Connect program reached 39.055 billion Hong Kong dollars last week, the highest weekly net inflow since April. ZhiPu (https://t.co/QtN5WBGUF7) attracted the largest net inflow at 13.702 billion Hong Kong dollars. Internet giants saw net buying overall, with Alibaba-W (https://t.co/UQPzf8o78D) receiving 8.318 billion Hong Kong dollars in net inflows, while Xiaomi Group (https://t.co/JRmn5zWzZn) saw net outflows of 802 million Hong Kong dollars.

Analysts forecast that China’s domestic procurement of AI computing chips will reach 1.44 trillion yuan by 2029, potentially giving rise to multiple listed companies with market capitalizations of one trillion or even ten trillion yuan. However, since July 1, the AI hardware sector has experienced a correction. Market analysts attribute this to a confluence of crowded positioning, capital reallocation, and profit-taking. Industry fundamentals remain intact, but internal sector differentiation and valuation adjustment will require time. The mid-July earnings season in both the U.S. and China will serve as a key test; companies delivering solid results are likely to stabilize first.

Leading fund managers including E Fund, Fuguo, Huaxia, and CUAM have recently increased their stakes in Hong Kong-listed pharmaceutical stocks. Coupled with policy benefits from the newly revised National Essential Medicines List, investment opportunities in the pharmaceutical sector have significantly strengthened. Several pharmaceutical fund managers note that the first half of the year saw fund flows driven by narratives around high industry vitality, technological innovation, and overseas expansion. Domestic innovative drug companies have now entered a cycle of technological breakthroughs and commercialized overseas sales. With current valuations at historical lows, the sector is poised to absorb capital flowing out of the technology sector.

According to Wind data, 360 A-share companies have released their 2026 first-half earnings forecasts, with 263 reporting positive outlooks—a 73.06% positive ratio. Overall profitability among companies that have disclosed forecasts remains strong.

China Industry News

In the first half of 2026, China’s outbound licensing of innovative drugs has already approached last year’s full-year total. Data shows that the National Medical Products Administration approved 38 innovative drugs in the first half of the year, 11 of which feature new targets and mechanisms and are domestically developed. The total transaction value of outbound licensing deals reached nearly $110 billion.

The 2026 International Low-Altitude Economy Expo will be held from July 22 to 25 at the National Exhibition and Convention Center (Shanghai). Startups including Volant and Fengfei Aviation will debut multiple eVTOL aircraft. The “Thousand Aircraft Display – 100 Low-Altitude Applications Flight Show” will showcase nearly 100 industry-grade aircraft and thousands of drone formations in real-world scenarios.

The film “Kung Fu Football Girls,” directed by Stephen Chow, has surpassed 500 million yuan in box office revenue. Maoyan Professional Box Office data has revised its total box office forecast upward, now projecting a final total of 2.5 billion yuan.

China has completed and commissioned 47 ultra-high-voltage transmission corridors. All 168 key power infrastructure projects under State Grid’s jurisdiction for the summer peak season have been completed on schedule, significantly enhancing cross-provincial transmission capacity and inter-regional power support.

As of July 9, 134 rural and township banks have been approved for exit in 2026, reducing the total number to 1,048, continuing the accelerated consolidation trend since 2025. Industry experts believe the county-level financial ecosystem is undergoing restructuring; reducing the number of rural banks will improve the quality and efficiency of financial services.

In the first half of 2026, the top 100 state-owned enterprise housing rental management entities collectively managed 2.011 million units, an increase of 239,000 units compared to the end of 2025, representing a 13.45% quarter-over-quarter growth. Market concentration has intensified: the top 10 firms manage 1.165 million units, accounting for 57.9% of the total.

Corporate News & Updates

Meta (META.O) has become embroiled in an AI-related controversy after launching a new feature that allows users to generate AI images using publicly available Instagram content by default. The feature faced widespread criticism, prompting Meta to remove it, citing failure to meet user expectations.

SpaceX stated that the 13th flight test of Starship is tentatively scheduled for July 16.

Samsung Electronics (005930.KS) has advanced the planned startup date for its first semiconductor factory in the Yongin chip cluster to 2029, accelerating the timeline by one to two years.

The World Artificial Intelligence Conference (WAIC 2026) will be held in Shanghai from July 17 to 20. Baidu (BIDU.O) AI will unveil its full suite of intelligent agents, showcasing the latest advancements in technology, application development, and industrial empowerment.

Xiaomi Auto stated that the release and market launch of its Pengcheng series models are progressing as planned, with further updates on timing expected soon. Regarding online speculation on pricing, Xiaomi Auto emphasized that some projected prices fall far below basic cost logic and urged the public to respect scientific and market principles.

Global Macro & Policy

Iranian Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Baghaei emphasized that future management arrangements for the Strait of Hormuz should be determined through consultations between Iran and Oman, taking into account recent U.S. and Israeli military actions against Iran and their impact on maritime security. Iran and Oman have agreed to continue political, legal, and technical consultations to reach a mutual understanding on maritime safety.

An Iranian military spokesperson stated that the U.S. must abide by the terms of the memorandum of understanding. U.S. attempts to establish “illegal shipping lanes” in the Strait of Hormuz have heightened regional instability. Iran’s armed forces will defend the rights of its citizens in the Strait.

WHO Emergency Director Chikwe Ihekweazu stated that the true scale of the Ebola outbreak in Africa may be significantly larger than official figures suggest, with many cases remaining undetected. The actual scale could be two to four times higher than reported.

Global Equity Markets

Japan plans to increase the proportion of unlisted equities, real estate, and other alternative investments in the Government Pension Investment Fund (GPIF), the world’s largest pension fund. Alternative investments accounted for only 1.7% of GPIF assets as of March, well below the 5% statutory limit. A government panel will soon issue a report recommending an increase to nearly 5%, aiming to broaden the fund’s asset base and reduce overall portfolio risk.

The Korea Composite Stock Price Index has risen approximately 80% year-to-date, repeatedly setting new highs, yet its forward price-to-earnings ratio stands at just 6.4, even below levels seen during the 2008 global financial crisis. Francis Tan, Asia Chief Strategist at Amundi Wealth Management Singapore, noted that suitability for investment depends on individual portfolio allocations; investors with low exposure to Korean equities may consider this a favorable entry point to gain exposure to AI-driven growth.

Bonds

Citic Securities (https://t.co/fLN5a98rLU) released a report noting that credit spreads in the third quarter typically narrow initially before widening again. After seasonal liquidity pressures subside in July, a window of opportunity may emerge, supported by continued demand from wealth management products and ETF expansion. A moderately aggressive allocation strategy is advisable, but investors should monitor potential adjustment pressures and rating revisions in the latter half of the quarter.

Commodities

The U.S. Central Command stated that since early May, U.S. forces have ensured the safe passage of over 800 commercial vessels and 400 million barrels of crude oil through the Strait of Hormuz.

FX & Currency Markets

SK Hynix (SKHY.O) raised approximately $26.5 billion through the issuance of American Depositary Receipts (ADRs) in the U.S. Upon completion of the offering, the proceeds will be paid to SK Hynix on July 14. The large influx of U.S. dollars will be converted into Korean won, significantly increasing dollar supply in the Korean foreign exchange market. Analysts believe this influx may help alleviate the prolonged weakening trend of the Korean won.

Global Financial Industry Daily Tracker
Date: Sunday, July 12, 2026 | Time: Morning Session
Coverage: Global Geopolitical & Financial Outlook
Executive Summary
Global financial markets are entering the new trading week with heightened caution. The dramatic collapse of the U.S.-Iran ceasefire agreement and subsequent military engagements in the Persian Gulf have fundamentally altered the commodity and risk-asset landscape. Markets are now pricing in a sustained "high-risk" premium, shifting focus from pure AI-speculation to defensive hedging and geopolitical energy resilience.
1. Geopolitical & Energy Crisis: Hormuz Remains Stalled
The Strait of Hormuz—the world’s most critical maritime energy artery—remains effectively paralyzed following the military escalation that began on July 9.
Maritime Logistical Impact: Global energy underwriters and shipping firms have largely halted transits through the Strait. Lloyd’s of London has increased war-risk insurance premiums for the Persian Gulf region by an additional 150% over the last 48 hours.
The Energy "Floor": Brent crude closed the week at approximately $78.20/bbl, marking a significant floor for oil prices. Energy analysts at Goldman Sachs and other desks are projecting that Brent could breach the $85/bbl mark early this week if diplomatic channels fail to reopen the waterway by Tuesday.
Global Strategic Reserve Status: With U.S. and European strategic petroleum reserves at multi-year lows, the market lacks a "buffer" to mitigate these physical supply disruptions, leaving energy prices highly sensitive to further regional escalations.
2. Global Equity & Treasury Outlook
The "AI-led" bull market, which defined the first half of 2026, is currently facing its stiffest test as institutional capital rotates into "Hard Asset" security.
Rotation to Defense: Equity strategists report a notable acceleration of capital out of highly levered "growth" technology stocks and into defensive sectors, particularly Aerospace & Defense, Global Utilities, and Food/Ag-Commodity producers.
Flight to Safety: The U.S. 10-Year Treasury yield is expected to open lower (trading at ~4.42% in pre-week indicative pricing) as investors prioritize the liquidity and stability of U.S. sovereign debt over corporate equity risk.
The "Warsh" Fed Context: Market focus remains fixed on how Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh will reconcile his "binary, data-dependent" monetary framework with the sudden supply-side energy shock. If energy prices continue to spike, the Fed may be forced into a more aggressive, inflation-fighting posture, even in the face of a softening labor market.
3. Notable Weekend Headlines
Iran’s Response: Over the weekend, Tehran signaled that it would seek "regional solidarity" to exert economic pressure on the West, raising concerns that the conflict may spread to involve pipeline infrastructure in neighboring Gulf states.
Corporate Tech Resilience: Despite the geopolitical friction, enterprise software remains a bright spot. Companies providing automated cybersecurity and AI-driven logistics optimization tools are seeing an uptick in inquiries, as global enterprises rush to harden their supply chains against digital and physical disruption.
Asian Indices Pre-Open: Futures markets in Tokyo and Seoul indicate a cautious start for Monday. Asian traders are particularly concerned about regional energy import costs, which are already showing up in inflationary forecasts for the third quarter.
4. Key Metrics to Watch (Monday, July 13)
Brent Crude Opening: Whether the price of oil gaps higher upon the reopening of Asian trading desks.
Defense Sector Movement: Tracking the price action of companies like Lockheed Martin, RTX Corp, and Northrop Grumman as they respond to the expanded defense authorization expectations.
Currency Volatility: Movement in the DXY (U.S. Dollar Index), which is acting as a major global anchor during the current period of flight-to-safety.

A sudden escalation in the Middle East has triggered a classic risk-off rotation this morning. While military commands contest the announced closure of a major shipping strait handling 20% of global seaborne oil, tanker traffic is slowing, driving tech down and sending energy premiums roaring back into the tape.

Crude oil spiked 4.0% to roughly $74.00, snapping a two-week decline after a regional power declared a key shipping lane closed.

Nasdaq futures fell 1.0% to 26,281.61 as crowded AI growth names bore the brunt of the selloff, while the S&P 500 slipped 0.4% to 7,575.39.

The 10-year Treasury yield jumped 4 basis points to 4.60% as oil-driven inflation fears resurfaced. Despite higher rates, safe-haven gold picked up 0.4% to $4,112, while$BTC dropped 0.9% to $63,800, moving in tandem with high-beta equities.

The Russel stalled out right at its critical 3,000 line, feeling downside pressure due to high concentration in tech-adjacent firms.

On Tuesday, July 14th, at 8:30 AM ET, Wall Street faces a high-volatility collision as marquee June CPI data drops simultaneously with major bank Q2 earnings.

Goldman Sachs is in the crosshairs with a $51.3 billion revenue forecast, where analysts will aggressively parse net interest income guidance to see if current rates have flipped from a tailwind to a burden.

If Tuesday's CPI print comes in hot alongside hawkish net interest income guidance from Goldman Sachs, the 10-year Treasury yield will violently break past 4.65%, instantly forcing the small-cap index to surrender its 3,000 baseline. Consequently, this macro double-whammy will likely trigger a sharp liquidity drain across high-multiple tech, dragging $BTC out of its consolidation pattern and flushing it down to test the $60,500 support level before mid-week.

Top Overnight News

The US and Iran exchanged fresh strikes while issuing conflicting declarations over whether the Strait of Hormuz is open to shipping. Oil up 3% BBG

Over the past several weeks, the investment-grade corporate bond market has struggled to absorb a combined $75 billion of bond issuance from NVDA, SPCX, and AMZN. That marks a shift from earlier in the year, when investors were generally happy to hand money to so-called AI hyperscalers by any possible means. WSJ

Warsh’s first big call will be whether or not to undo last year’s cuts. A steadier economy and stubborn inflation have put a rate increase in play. The new chairman, who testifies this week, hasn’t tipped his hand. WSJ

Three prominent artificial intelligence developers released new models over the past week. They all promise to be more advanced, but their biggest immediate selling point may not be what they can do but how little they charge to do it. BBG

China’s crude imports look poised to recover from a months-long slump as the country relaxes fuel export curbs, raises run rates and snaps up prompt Middle East supplies, with analysts and traders forecasting a return to strategic stockpiling later this year. BBG

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co reported a 67.9% year-on-year rise in its June sales on Monday, ahead of its second-quarter earnings release later this week. CNBC

The yen weakened after Reuters reported that Japan has no plans to overhaul the GPIF’s asset allocation. Chief Cabinet Secretary Minoru Kihara said the GPIF routinely undertakes an appropriate review of its portfolio and will make amendments if required. BBG

Meta plans to spend an additional $40 billion on its data center campus in Louisiana, bringing total costs to above $250 billion. BBG

Companies from Silicon Valley to Europe are turning to Chinese AI models as they try to cut the cost of using the technology and reduce their dependence on US frontier labs. FT

The distribution of investor views surrounding the path of monetary policy in coming months is wide. Goldman economists’ baseline forecast is that the FOMC will leave the policy rate unchanged this year but they assign a 25% probability to a scenario where the Fed hikes. Market pricing is more hawkish, reflecting a base case of nearly 50 bp of hikes through mid-2027, with uncertainty around that outlook. Option pricing signals greater than a 50% likelihood of hikes, but also substantial probabilities to scenarios where the Fed cuts or remains on hold. Conversations with clients reflect a similarly wide range of expectations. Investors also express an unusually wide distribution of views regarding the implications of any given Fed policy path for equities. Goldman

Goldman upgrades oil prices forecast on higher structural security premium

Goldman Sachs raised its long-dated oil price forecast by $9/b to $76/b, citing a higher structural security premium due to ongoing U.S.-Iran tensions and Strait of Hormuz.

Recent tanker attacks and strikes highlight short-term vulnerability, supporting higher prices. However, the bank sees downside risk from new Middle East pipelines expected to bypass Hormuz, insulating over 45% of Gulf exports by end-2027 and 60%+ by 2028.

Goldman on CPI:

We expect a 0.17% increase in June core CPI (vs. +0.3% consensus), corresponding to a year-over-year rate of +2.76% (vs. +2.9% consensus). We expect a 0.11% decline in headline CPI (vs. -0.1% consensus), reflecting lower energy prices. Our forecast is consistent with a 0.24% increase in core PCE in June, reflecting another large increase in its financial services component.

We highlight four key component-level trends we expect to see in the report:
-we expect weak autos inflation
-we forecast benign readings for the shelter categories
-we expect more moderate increases in the travel services categories 
-we expect downward pressure from potential residual seasonality.

We expect monthly core CPI increases around 0.2% over the next couple of months, reflecting the continued slowdown in the shelter categories and the reversal of upward pressure from higher jet fuel prices and the World Cup on the travel services categories, though risks are tilted to the upside if disruptions to oil markets and associated oil price increases prove more persistent than expected.

Just in: Overseas Research and Selection Daily 0713: Goldman Sachs: The inflationary impact of the Middle East conflict has weakened, and the Federal Reserve is expected to keep interest rates unchanged during the year.

Goldman Sachs: The AI boom may push South Korea's exports to exceed one trillion U.S. dollars, and Korean won stability will become the key to growth spillovers Goldman Sachs’ latest research report states that South Korea is becoming the main beneficiary of the global AI boom.

Driven by the surge in memory chip prices, exports are expected to exceed US$1 trillion in 2026. In the first five months, South Korea's memory chip exports increased by nearly 150% year-on-year, about 80% of which came from price increases; semiconductors contributed three-quarters of the increase in exports as of May.

Rising export prices have significantly improved South Korea's terms of trade, rising 8.6% year-on-year in the first quarter and further rising to about 20% in the second quarter. Superimposed on real GDP growth of 3.8%, South Korea's nominal GDP increased by 17.1% year-on-year in the first quarter, the fastest growth rate in 30 years.

Cross-border experience shows that improvements in trade terms usually promote currency appreciation, lower inflation and capital expenditure growth in manufacturing exporting countries; for every 10% improvement in trade terms, the actual capital expenditures of manufacturing exporting countries usually increase by 0.9%.

However, the South Korean won has weakened recently, bucking the trend, which may weaken the boost of AI boom to domestic demand. Goldman Sachs estimates that a 5% depreciation of the Korean won will push up inflation by about 30 basis points and suppress residents' purchasing power and local corporate profits.

At the same time, the weak Korean won's boost to exports has been reduced due to the expansion of overseas production.

If the South Korean government's "three major super projects" are implemented according to the previous semiconductor industry cluster model, the actual capital expenditure growth rate in the next few years can increase by 2 to 3 percentage points annually, the annual GDP growth rate will increase by 0.4 percentage points, and the scale of related public and private investment may increase to 3 to 4 percentage points of GDP.

Goldman Sachs recommends that South Korea prioritize stabilizing the won in the short term, gradually tighten monetary policy in the medium term, and save and reallocate part of its fiscal earnings through a proposed "future response fund." Goldman Sachs: The inflationary impact of the Middle East conflict is weakening, and the Fed is expected to keep

interest rates unchanged during the year Goldman Sachs said that the United States and Iran have recently launched attacks on each other again, and shipping risks in the Strait of Hormuz have increased, but the impact on commodity prices caused by the war has significantly receded.

Oil prices have only rebounded modestly and are still about 30% lower than their highs from the end of April to early May; U.S. gasoline prices fell by 15%, aviation fuel prices fell by 35%, and most Gulf export commodities such as methanol, polyethylene and nitrogen fertilizer are close to pre-war levels.

Although sea and air transportation costs continue to rise, international transportation only accounts for 1% to 2% of the import cost of consumer goods in the United States, and its impact on terminal prices is limited.

Although Gulf crude oil flows have declined, they are still above wartime lows, and global visible oil inventories are not at unusually low levels. The Goldman Sachs model shows that the incremental impact of the conflict on core PCE quarter-on-quarter inflation has peaked in the second quarter, especially from May to June.

If the situation does not escalate, the related pressure will weaken significantly in the third and fourth quarters. Supply shortages and supply chain pressures are far lower than during the epidemic, and current inflation expectations have not been significantly anchored.

The marginal impact of superimposed tariffs and AI demand on monthly inflation has subsided. Goldman Sachs expects core PCE to increase by 0.24% month-on-month in June and then maintain at 0.20% to 0.23%; the year-on-year growth rate in August will fall by 0.2 percentage points to 3.2% due to adjustments to statistical methods.

The bank expects the Federal Reserve to keep interest rates unchanged for the rest of 2026, but the policy room for error is limited.

If the conflict escalates again and pushes oil prices back to US$100 per barrel, core inflation may rise an additional 3 to 4 basis points month-on-month in the coming months and significantly increase the risk of interest rate hikes.

Goldman Sachs: Middle East countries are accelerating the expansion of pipelines bypassing Hormuz, which may cover more than 60% of Gulf oil exports Goldman Sachs said that attacks on oil tankers and the resumption of military operations by the United States and Iran have driven up the recent rise in oil prices, indicating that the Strait of Hormuz remains the most critical source of risk for short-term oil prices.

If the conflict seriously escalates, uncertainty about Gulf exports will re-amplify the risk of upward oil prices. But in the long term, Middle East oil-producing countries are accelerating the construction and restoration of oil pipelines that bypass the Strait of Hormuz.

Goldman Sachs looked at 9 major pipelines and found that the median construction time of historical projects was 2.5 years; supply disruptions usually speed up project progress, but projects involving multi-national coordination take longer. Goldman Sachs evaluated seven pipeline projects that are in the construction, planning or potential expansion stages.

Under the baseline scenario, the effective oil transportation capacity bypassing Hormuz will increase by 3.8 million barrels per day by the end of 2027, with a cumulative increase of 7.3 million barrels per day by the end of 2028, with a total scale of more than 14 million barrels per day, which can guarantee more than 60% of the pre-war 23 million barrels per day export volume of Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, Iraq, Qatar, Bahrain, the United Arab Emirates and Iran.

The total project cost is expected to be US$30 billion to US$48 billion. By the end of 2027, the proportion of protected exports can exceed 45%. If construction speeds up, the coverage ratio will reach 75% by the end of 2028; under a conservative scenario, it will be slightly higher than 45%.

Goldman Sachs previously raised its three-year Brent crude oil price assumption by US$9 to US$76 per barrel, reflecting a higher structural safety premium, but the bypass pipeline expansion may pose a downside risk to long-term oil prices.

Bank of America: Global cloud capital expenditure will reach US$1.15 trillion in 2027, and the AI investment cycle remains sustainable The latest research report from Bank of America Securities predicts that the capital expenditures of the world's largest cloud vendors will reach US$197 billion in the second quarter, a year-on-year increase of 30% and a

year-on-year increase of 83%; capital expenditures in 2026 and 2027 will increase to US$851 billion and US$1.15 trillion respectively, a year-on-year increase of 78% and 35%, significantly higher than the May forecast. Bank of America believes that investment in AI infrastructure is sustainable.

The remaining performance obligations and order backlog disclosed by Microsoft, Amazon, Google and Oracle total more than $2 trillion, providing contract demand support for construction from 2026 to 2028.

At the same time, AI commercialization is accelerating: OpenAI's revenue is expected to increase from US$13 billion in 2025 to US$62 billion in 2027, and Anthropic's annual revenue has exceeded US$47 billion as of May 2026; the number of tokens generated by the agent workload can reach 10 to 1,000 times that of ordinary conversations, further increasing the demand for computing power.

High investment will still squeeze cash flow. Bank of America predicts that cloud vendor capital expenditures will reach approximately 100% to 115% of operating cash flow from 2026 to 2028, and industry free cash flow profit margins will drop to -1%, -5%, and -4% respectively, far lower than the historical 15% to 20%.

However, related expenses can basically be covered by operating cash flow. Since 2026, the five major cloud vendors have raised approximately US$244 billion, mainly long-term debt and permanent equity from 3 to 40 years. Bank of America believes that this is a balance sheet optimization and not a signal of financial pressure.

The bank is optimistic about Nvidia, Broadcom, Marvell, AMD, Micron and the semiconductor equipment industry chain.

Goldman Sachs: AI supply chain accelerates production expansion in Southeast Asia, China’s manufacturing efficiency advantage is still difficult to replace After visiting 10 PCB, network equipment and AI server supply chain companies in Thailand and Vietnam, Goldman Sachs said that relevant manufacturers are expanding production capacity, and the new

projects are mainly for AI servers, CPO switches, optical modules, AI PCB and edge AI equipment, reflecting the continued strong demand for AI infrastructure and the diversification of production bases driven by customers.

Southeast Asia is forming industrial clusters divided by customer ecology: Vietnam mainly undertakes the supply chains of Microsoft, Dell and Apple, Thailand focuses on Google and HP, and Malaysia gathers Amazon suppliers. However, mainland China remains a manufacturing center with its complete supply chain, mature experience and production efficiency.

Labor costs in Thailand and Vietnam are low, but efficiency is also low. The output of a single Chinese worker can be equivalent to up to three Thai workers. If raw materials such as copper-clad laminates are imported from China, tariffs and transportation may push up costs by 15% to 20%.

However, in order to spread geographical risks, customers are willing to accept a cost premium of up to about 20%, and manufacturers are improving efficiency through automation, generative AI and digitalization.

Supported by customer prepayments, equipment subsidies, and demand from cloud vendors, model developers, sovereign AI, and enterprises, Goldman Sachs continues to be optimistic about the AI capital expenditure cycle and has raised its global AI server chip shipment forecasts from 2026 to 2028 by 14%, 22%, and 14% respectively. U.S.

cloud vendors have raised their capital expenditure forecasts by 8%, 27%, and 25%, and Chinese cloud vendors have raised their forecasts by 37%, 44%, and 55%.

Jefferies: Research and development of weight-loss drugs has shifted to muscle-protection and new long-acting drug delivery mechanisms have become the focus of competition Jefferies' latest research report stated that obesity drug research and development is shifting from simply pursuing higher weight loss to improving the quality of weight loss, durability of efficacy and convenience of medication.

Differentiated oral platforms, ultra-long-acting preparations and non-incretin mechanisms have become the focus of capital and industrial layout. ALN1003 developed by Alaunos Therapeutics is an oral non-incretin small molecule. Animal data further shows that it may improve liver indicators and insulin resistance.

However, the company only has about US$350,000 in cash, and financing is the key to advancing clinical applications.

The ASC36 and ASC36_35 compound preparations developed by Ascletis Pharma have submitted clinical applications to the US FDA, targeting monthly or quarterly administration, and monthly combined administration respectively; the half-life of ASC36 in animal testing is approximately 6 times that of similar drugs.

SKNY-1, developed by MIRA Pharmaceuticals, has pharmacokinetic data supporting once-daily oral administration and targets simultaneous appetite, reward and metabolic pathways.

The anti-Activin E antibody "610" developed by the American biotechnology company iBio was combined with semaglutide to reduce lean body mass loss by 73%; RES-010 animal data showed that fat was reduced by about 15%, lean body mass was only reduced by 1%, and the semaglutide group decreased by 8%, and there was no obvious rebound after drug withdrawal.

Jefferies: The U.S. packaged food industry is under pressure due to consumer dissatisfaction, regulatory risks and rising raw material prices. Jefferies' latest research report states that the U.S.

packaged food industry is facing triple pressure: consumer dissatisfaction with food prices is increasing, health and reputation risks caused by ultra-processed foods are rising, and the El Niño phenomenon is pushing up the cost of raw materials such as cocoa and coffee again.

A survey covering 1,100 registered voters showed that 67% of respondents were under significant cost of living pressure, 63% listed groceries as their biggest source of stress, and 67% believed food prices were "unfair." 48% attributed the price increase to tariffs and trade restrictions, 46% believed that companies raised prices to increase profits; 82% believed that the political sector could reduce costs by taking priority actions.

Majorities of Democratic, Republican and independent voters support the government's crackdown on price gouging, even as it increases regulations and puts packaged food companies' pricing strategies under greater political scrutiny. In terms of health risks, the U.S.

Federal Court dismissed an ultra-processed food lawsuit against 11 companies including Kraft Heinz, Mondelēz, Post, General Mills and Nestlé on June 30, on the grounds that the plaintiff failed to prove that the specific company or product had a causal relationship with type 2 diabetes and fatty liver disease.

However, the court acknowledged that the case involved children's health issues, indicating that consumers' concerns about food ingredients are still rising, and the reputational risks of the industry are difficult to subside. On the cost side, New York cocoa futures have risen to about $6,000 per ton.

Jefferies pointed out that heavy rainfall in West Africa and the confirmed El Niño have increased the risk of cocoa diseases and production reductions, and coffee price expectations have also been raised. Its commodity index predicts that the input cost of packaged food will increase by 3% year-on-year in the second half of 2026.

Goldman Sachs first commented on Wisdom and assigned a "neutral" rating with a target price of HK$1,880.

Goldman Sachs covered Knowledge Atlas Technology, the parent company of Zhipu AI, for the first time, giving it a "neutral" rating, with a 12-month target price of HK$1,880, corresponding to a valuation of US$110 billion; the optimistic and pessimistic scenario valuations are US$200 billion and US$54 billion respectively.

Goldman Sachs believes that Zhipu is one of the most competitive manufacturers in China's enterprise-level and programming large model fields. The flagship model GLM-5.2 is close to the global cutting-edge level, ranking second and third in the world in Code Arena and real task agent benchmarks respectively.

A large number of real programming tasks form a data feedback loop, which is expected to support the company in maintaining an iteration speed of one small version every 1 to 2 months and one major version every 7 to 8 months.

Goldman Sachs predicts that the company’s API business’ annualized recurring revenue will reach US$1.5 billion by the end of 2026, which is higher than the company’s US$1 billion target; due to computing power constraints, potential demand is about 2 to 3 times the current supply.

Revenues from 2026 to 2028 are expected to be 6.3 billion yuan, 23.7 billion yuan and 58.8 billion yuan respectively, but there will still be a net loss of approximately 1.3 billion yuan in 2028.

Since the company's listing, the company's stock price has increased by approximately 1,600% (as of the release of the report), the effective circulating share ratio is only approximately 5%, and the current valuation is equivalent to 80 times the expected ARR by the end of 2026.

Goldman Sachs believes that the valuation has basically reflected the leading premium; in the long term, if it shifts from completely open source to an open weight model with commercial terms, revenue and profit margins are expected to be further released.

Major risks include industry competition, high R&D investment, continued losses and Sino-US technological friction.

Goldman Sachs: AI demand drives up ABF substrate gap, market size may reach US$33 billion in 2028 The latest research report from Goldman Sachs stated that the demand for AI servers and general server CPUs is growing faster than expected, and ABF carrier boards may become a new bottleneck in the AI and server industry chain.

The bank has significantly raised its forecast for the compound growth rate of the ABF carrier board market from 33% to 62% from 2025 to 2028, and the market size is expected to increase from US$7.7 billion in 2025 to US$33 billion in 2028. The tight supply and demand has been advanced to the second quarter of 2026.

Goldman Sachs predicts that the ABF carrier board shortage rate will expand from 8% in 2026 to 34% in 2027 and 51% in 2028; if calculated in the second half of the year, the gap in the second half of 2026 will be approximately 14%. The current delivery cycle has been extended from 3 to 4 months at the beginning of the year to more than 12 months.

The industry may continue to be at full production until 2028, and most of the new production capacity will not be released until the second half of 2028. On the demand side, the proportion of AI servers and general servers in total ABF demand is expected to increase from 25% in 2025 to 62% in 2028.

Among them, the compound growth rate of AI server-related demand from 2025 to 2028 is expected to reach 101%, and the proportion will rise to 45% in 2028; the demand for general server CPUs is driven by intelligent AI, and the compound growth rate is expected to be 47%, and the proportion will rise to 17%.

Specification upgrades also increase the carrier board consumption of a single chip. The ABF area required by Nvidia's Rubin GPU is expected to increase by 123% compared to Blackwell, and the Vera CPU requires an increase of 47% compared to Grace.

Goldman Sachs predicts that from the third quarter of 2026 to at least the end of 2027, long-term agreement prices and spot prices will increase by 10% to 15% and more than 20% each quarter respectively, and spot prices may increase by 60% to 80% year-on-year in 2027 and 2028.

The bank expects the second-quarter profits of four carrier board factories in Taiwan to be 4% to 38% higher than market expectations, and raised the target prices of Nanya Circuit Board, Jingshuo, Xinxing and Zhending to NT$2310, 1120, 1140 and NT$824 respectively, maintaining a "buy" rating for the remaining three companies except Xinxing. Overview of the new week’s important schedule Warsh attended the Congressional hearing for the first time as Chairman of the Federal Reserve, and the market focused on interest rate signals throughout the week. Federal Reserve Chairman Warsh will attend Congress's semi-annual monetary policy hearings for two consecutive days on Tuesday and Wednesday.

This is the first time that Warsh has gone to Congress as chairman of the Federal Reserve to testify on monetary policy since taking office, and it will also become the most important trading event of the week.

At 22:00 on Tuesday (this article is Beijing time), Warsh will attend a hearing of the House of Representatives Financial Services Committee; on Wednesday at 22:00, he will transfer to the Senate Banking, Housing and Urban Affairs Committee to testify. Both hearings focused on the Fed's semiannual monetary policy report to Congress.

The biggest highlight of this hearing is that the U.S. June CPI released at 20:30 on Tuesday is only 90 minutes away from the first hearing. Warsh can hardly avoid the issues of inflation and interest rate paths.

Markets will focus on how he evaluates the latest inflation data, whether the shock to energy prices is sustainable, and whether he will leave open the possibility of a rate hike in July or September.

The June FOMC statement deleted traditional forward guidance on interest rates, and Warsh later emphasized that the Fed will make judgments based on data and economic prospects. If CPI cools down and Warsh weakens his hawkish stance, U.S.

bond yields and the US dollar may face downward pressure, and technology growth stocks and gold are expected to gain support; if inflation exceeds expectations and Warsh does not rule out further tightening of policy, the market may continue to strengthen interest rate hike trades. In addition, many Fed officials will speak publicly.

At 00:30 on Tuesday, Fed Governor Waller gave a speech. At 01:00 on Wednesday, 2027 FOMC Voting Committee and Chicago Fed President Goolsby participated in the fireside chat; at 20:45, FOMC Permanent Voting Committee and New York Fed President Williams delivered a speech.

At 06:30 on Thursday, Mussallem, the 2028 FOMC voting committee member and President of the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis, delivered a speech. At 00:30 on Friday, 2026 FOMC voting committee and Dallas Fed President Logan delivered a speech; at 07:00, Federal Reserve Vice Chairman Jefferson delivered a speech on economic and monetary policy. The U.S.

CPI and PPI in June were released one after another, and inflation and consumption data tested expectations for interest rate hikes. The U.S. June CPI and core CPI will be released at 20:30 on Tuesday. Many institutions predict that the overall U.S.

If core inflation falls significantly, the market may reduce its bets on raising interest rates within the year; conversely, if rising energy prices begin to spread to the prices of goods and services, expectations for further tightening of policy by the Federal Reserve may increase. At 20:30 on Wednesday, the U.S.

June PPI and core PPI will take over, providing investors with the latest clues to upstream price pressures. The U.S. June retail sales released at 20:30 on Thursday will test the impact of high interest rates and rising prices on household consumption.

Just in: Wall Street earns huge commissions from Space X IPO, big M&A deals.

Driven by the blockbuster listing of SpaceX and the rebound in giant mergers and acquisitions, major Wall Street investment banks will report their highest investment banking commission income in four and a half years this week. The commission income from investment banking business of the five leading U.S.

investment banks, Citigroup and Citigroup, is expected to increase by 27% year-on-year in the second quarter. The total commission scale may reach US$11.1 billion, setting a new high since the industry’s phased peak in 2021. A large part of the commission increase comes from the equity capital markets business.

The total revenue of this sector of the above-mentioned five major investment banks is expected to reach US$2.5 billion. The core driver is the US$500 million in underwriting commissions brought by the SpaceX IPO project to 23 partner investment banks. This is also the highest listing underwriting fee in the world's history.

Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley each received $100 million from the deal.

Brian Mulberry, chief market strategist at Zacks Investment Management, said: "The volume of SpaceX's listing is huge, and we expect that all investment banks participating in the underwriting will benefit significantly." Bankers and analysts have pointed out that a number of large technology companies have completed listings or are about to enter the capital market, including SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic.

However, affected by the high interest rate environment, the listing activities of small and medium-sized enterprises owned by private equity institutions have been relatively deserted.

Commission income from the M&A business of the five major investment banks is expected to surge by about 30% year-on-year, with the total exceeding US$4 billion; this is also the first time since 2021 that commissions in this sector have stood at the US$4 billion mark for three consecutive quarters.

Wall Street investment banks have benefited greatly from the rebound in large-scale mergers and acquisitions worth more than US$10 billion. Morgan Stanley mentioned in a client research report this week that the scale of global disclosed M&A transactions is expected to hit a record high in 2026.

JPMorgan Chase, Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, Bank of America, Citibank and Both are scheduled to release financial reports on Tuesday, with Morgan Stanley delaying its disclosure to Wednesday. The market expects the combined net profit of these six banks to be approximately US$44 billion in the second quarter, a year-on-year increase of 18%.

The trading departments of various banks will also benefit from the increasingly volatile financial markets and provide customers with transaction matching and financing services. Among them, the growth of stock trading business is particularly eye-catching.

While the investment banking sector achieved growth, losses in the credit business remained at a relatively moderate level, supporting the rise of bank stocks. The banking sector as a whole has outperformed the market in the past two years. This has also led the market to question how much room there is for bank stocks to rise in the future.

Saul Martinez, head of U.S. financial industry equity research at HSBC, said: "The threshold for market expectations has been raised. This quarter's performance should be good, but the market has already predicted this, and the stock price trend has already reflected this." Investors will also scrutinize bank balance sheets for signs of stress on U.S.

residents. At present, in the face of various shocks such as import tariffs, conflicts between the United States and Iran, and rising oil prices, American consumers are relatively resistant to risks. Martinez said: "The real cause for concern is that the conflict escalates again, oil prices rebound sharply, and inflation returns.

Only then will economic growth become worrying."

text:
“存储双雄”又暴跌！📉韩国央行竟然这么说

一、核心政策表态：韩国央行明确周期未到顶
韩国央行周一发布报告反驳市场 “芯片周期见顶” 悲观预期，核心观点：
当前全球半导体供需格局依旧供不应求，AI 基建持续拉动存储需求，但高端 HBM 产能扩产受工艺、良率约束，供给释放速度远慢于需求；
本轮周期和传统存储复苏完全不同，底层逻辑是 AI 全面渗透带来产业变革，云厂商持续资本开支具备长期持续性；
HBM 等定制化高端存储生产壁垒高、扩产周期长，行业高景气扩张将维持较长时间；
多家海外大行（大摩、小摩、高盛）一致预判，至少 2027 年前半导体需求保持强劲。

二、盘面反差：利好发声当日，存储龙头大幅杀跌
消息落地当天韩股走弱，存储双雄同步暴跌：
SK 海力士大跌近 8%，三星电子跌近 4%；
近期调整幅度显著：7 月 2 日三星大跌 9%、SK 海力士跌近 15%；即便三星公布二季度近 599 亿美元超预期利润，股价依旧高开低走大跌 7%。

三、股价下跌四大核心矛盾
1. 资金提前博弈远期供给过剩（核心分歧）
短期 HBM 订单锁满、现货紧缺是共识，但市场担忧三星、SK 海力士持续加码先进产线，2027-2028 年集中扩产落地后，存储供需反转；周期板块资金习惯提前计价远期利空，高位估值先行消化。

2. 高位获利盘集中兑现
上半年 HBM 行情驱动存储股大幅上涨，7 月进入机构半年净值结算窗口，海外公募、对冲基金集中止盈减仓，抛压集中释放。

3. 基本面结构性割裂，估值存在隐忧
景气高度集中于 AI 服务器 HBM，手机、PC 等消费端存储需求持续疲软；市场担忧 AI 资本开支依赖举债，行业估值高位、一旦云厂商收缩 capex，存储需求弹性快速回落。

4. 中东地缘风险压制风险偏好
霍尔木兹海峡通行受限，双重冲击韩系存储产业链：
1）韩国 80% 原油、大量 LNG 经海峡进口，能源涨价抬升晶圆厂电力成本；
2）卡塔尔供给全球近 30% 电子级氦气，氦气是光刻、刻蚀必备原料，物流受阻带来特种气体涨价、供应短缺隐忧，压制芯片板块风险偏好。

四、行情核心矛盾总结
          中长期产业逻辑（央行 / 机构）：AI 算力持续扩容，HBM 工艺壁垒高、扩产受限，存储超级周期延续；短期订单饱满、长协锁定业绩，基本面韧性充足。
         短期交易逻辑（二级市场）：股价上半年涨幅巨大，资金提前博弈远期产能过剩 + 半年报止盈离场 + 中东地缘避险，引发估值回调。
         结构性机会：行情分化加剧，仅高端 HBM、存储设备、国产存储材料具备持续逻辑，普通消费类存储承压。

五、后续跟踪关键观察点
HBM4 价格走势、大厂长期供货协议签署情况；
三星、SK 海力士新增产能爬坡进度，供给释放节奏；
云厂商 AI 资本开支指引是否下调；
霍尔木兹海峡通航、氦气等电子特气供应链变化；
国内长鑫科技上市带来存储板块估值重塑。

text:
战争在打，能定价的市场却没开门 —— CME 重开前的最后 3 小时（07-12）

⭐ 一句话：过去三个小时中东连续升级，而 $BTC 一动没动 —— 34 美元。今早报 $64,135，现在 $64,169。

期间发生了什么：伊朗革命卫队正式宣布关闭霍尔木兹海峡、伊朗导弹/无人机打了六个国家的美军基地、科威特一座海上石油钻井平台被炸、美军刚刚打掉霍尔木兹周边的反舰导弹与防空系统。

别把这个「没反应」读成「市场认为无所谓」。真相是：能给这些消息定价的市场（原油、黄金、美股期货）现在根本没开门，而 crypto 是 0.35 倍地量的周末薄盘。定价不会不发生，只是被推迟到 —— 今天 15:00（加州时间）CME 重开，距现在不到 3 小时。

🌍 一、宏观：三天的战争，一分钱都还没定价
美股、原油、黄金全部休市，第二天了。屏幕上挂着的还是周五收盘锚：纳指 26,281.61（+0.29%）｜标普 7,575.39（+0.42%）｜道指 52,637.01（+0.29%）｜费半 12,967.16（+0.06%）｜NVDA $210.96（+4.03%）领涨｜VIX 15.03｜WTI 原油 $71.41｜黄金 $4,113.70。

这一整排数字全部是【战前定价】—— 周五收盘时，海峡还没「关」、六国美军基地还没挨打、科威特油井还没被炸。它们已经过期了。今天 15:00 CME 重开 = 油/金/美股期货一次性补定价三天地缘，这是本周第一个、也是最大的跳空窗口。

🛢️ 二、国际形势：「关了」和「没关」同时是真的
升级是真的：美军一周内第三轮空袭伊朗（约 140 个目标），阿巴斯港、格什姆岛均遭袭；伊朗弹道导弹反击卡塔尔、阿曼、巴林、科威特、约旦、阿联酋六国境内美军目标；伊朗波斯湾海峡管理局称海峡「目前无法通行」，革命卫队正式宣布关闭。

但美国中央司令部、特朗普、联合海上信息中心三方同时否认：海峡是国际水道、南部（阿曼一侧）航道双向通行正常、美军护航就位。

谁在说真话？看船，不看嘴 —— 过去 24 小时仅 11 艘商船通过（8 油轮 + 3 货船），正常日均约 34 艘、峰值 59 艘。独立研究机构 HFI Research 最新观察也对得上：伊朗一侧航道仍有船只，但整体航运十分冷清。

👉 结论：这不是「封锁」，是【通行量砍掉三分之二的争议性半关闭】。差别很大 —— 完全封锁油价是跳空级，打折封锁只是溢价级。

❗ 但今天有两处质变：
1）科威特国家石油公司一座海上钻井平台被无人机炸伤 —— 此前挨打的都是船只和军事目标，这是第一个被直接击中的能源生产设施。供给端的性质从「运输受阻」变成了「产能受损」。
2）美军成体系打击了霍尔木兹周边的反舰导弹阵地、防空系统、革命卫队快艇 —— 问题从「伊朗想不想关海峡」变成了「伊朗还有没有能力关」。方向是拧着的：打击进行中 = 短期利多油价；封锁能力被逐步摧毁 = 中期把溢价往回压。这两股力量会在 15:00 CME 开盘时正面打架，别想当然认定只有一个方向。

⚠️ 还有一处要升级理解：伊朗议员今日明说「今后霍尔木兹海峡的管理将由伊朗方面安排，这是伊朗的【最终】政策」。伊朗要的不是一次临时封锁，是改掉海峡的【管理制度】。如果这成真，油价里要长期含一块结构性溢价，而不是打一波就回落的脉冲。

同时谈判在加速：阿曼「双航道」方案（南航道自由通行 / 北航道需伊朗批准、不收费）；伊阿同意继续技术+政治谈判、卡塔尔参与；伊拉克总理周一访华盛顿签油气协议 + 重建绕开霍尔木兹的输油管（需 2-3 年）。

📊 三、技术面（强弱：ETH ＞ BTC ＞ SOL）
🔵 BTC $64,169（24h -0.17%）｜区间 63,605–64,439
　1H 站上 MA20/MA50（64,010 / 64,081），1H MACD 柱转正（+47）= 极微弱的企稳
　但 4H MACD 柱仍为负（-64），动能没回来；日线 RSI 53.5、MACD 零轴下方金叉
　🔴 分水岭：日线 MA50 = 64,670（昨晚 64,944、前天 65,136 —— 均线在往下走，压顶线自己在下移来找价格）

🟢 ETH $1,820（-0.16%，仍最强）｜区间 1,778–1,829.5
　稳稳站在日线 MA50（1,758）上方｜日线 RSI 58.6、MACD 柱 +43｜4H/1H 全在均线上方

🔴 SOL $77.53（-0.64%，最弱）｜区间 75.58–78.30
　4H 跌破 MA20（77.85）和 MA50（79.25）= 空头排列，RSI 45.5

⭐ 成交量才是今天最要命的一格：BTC 最近一根完整日线成交量只有 20 日均量的 0.35 倍；盘中 1H 量比 BTC 0.45、ETH 0.59、SOL 0.42 —— 地量在加深，不是在恢复。
🧭 换句话说：不是「BTC 冷静地消化了战争」，是【房间里根本没人】。周末薄盘里的价格不是信息，这三个小时的「稳」不能当成「市场消化了战争」来读。

⚙️ 四、衍生品结构
资金费率（8小时）：BTC +0.0088% / +0.0061%（温和偏多）｜ETH 【-0.0037%】（比今早更负）/ +0.0020%｜SOL +0.0050%
未平仓合约（OI）24h 几乎没动：BTC $471.5 亿（+0.19%）｜ETH $251.9 亿（+0.03%）｜SOL $50.5 亿（-0.62%，唯一减仓）
24h 爆仓：BTC $1,780 万（多头 1.89 : 1 被打）｜ETH $3,434 万（多 1.18 : 1）｜SOL $1,105 万（多头 10 : 1 被血洗）

⭐ 本段最重要的变化：ETH 那台「轧空发动机」的燃料基本烧完了 —— 前天空头还被轧 4.8:1，今天已经反过来是多头在挨打。ETH 还是最强的，但托着它上涨的强制平仓买盘正在消失。SOL 是反面：多头在被单边收割。

🎯 五、BTC 核心指标
🔴 现货溢价：-$41.4 / -0.0645%（今早 -$39.5）—— 不但没转正，还略微走阔。美国机构现货买盘持续缺席，这条线一天不转正，反弹就一天缺主力。
🔴 恐惧贪婪指数：26（恐惧），与今早持平
🔵 BTC DVOL（隐含波动率）36.44 ｜ ETH DVOL 51.08
⭐ 隐含 vs 已实现（纠正一个想当然）：BTC 隐含 36.3 vs 【7 日已实现 34.9】= 只贵了 1.4 个点，波动率并不便宜；只有跟 30 日已实现 42.1 比才显得低。
　👉 所以别用「波动率白菜价」当买期权的理由。真正的理由是【窗口里塞着两颗有日期的炸弹】（今天 CME 重开、周二 CPI），而隐含波动率是按过去两周的平静定价的。
🔵 MaxPain：7/13 = 63,500（PCR 2.03，put 明显偏重）｜7/14 = 63,500｜7/15-16 = 64,000

🧭 六、综合判断 + 交易建议
核心矛盾：一场正在打的战争 vs 一组还没定价的市场 —— 而 crypto 在一个空房间里独自报价。
⏳ 核心纪律：距 15:00 CME 重开不到 3 小时。【地量 + 一个已知的跳空窗口 = 现在开单边是赌头条，不是交易】。想表达波动就用跨式（7/15–7/17 到期），不赌方向，也不怕「打谈交替」来回反转。

👉 BTC：分水岭仍是日线 MA50 【64,670】
　· 破 63,600 才做空（止损 64,700，目标 62,400 → 61,500）
　· 放量站上 64,670 且现货溢价转正 → 空头框架作废，看 68,000–70,000；溢价不转正就当假突破
　· 没破位不空，没站上不追。中间这段就是不该有仓位的地方。

👉 ETH：最强、站稳 1,758 偏多，但轧空燃料烧完 + 费率转更负，别追高。回踩 1,780–1,800 才是轻多的位置，目标 1,860。

👉 SOL：最弱，4H 已转空排，多头被单边割 10:1 —— 想做多别选它。

⚠️ 方向别想当然：新闻显示这是【半关闭 + 谈判在加速】，不是全面封锁 → 油的跳空幅度大概率小于「海峡关闭」这个标题所暗示的。而且上一轮升级（7/9）黄金是【跌】的（油涨→通胀→加息重定价→压金价）。别指望「数字黄金」叙事这次会救 BTC。

· 今天 15:00（加州）—— CME Globex 重开，油/金/美股期货补定价三天地缘，本周最大跳空窗口
· 地缘尾部：「打谈交替」是固定节奏（打→谈→打→缓和），若周内传出「伊朗同意重开海峡」，追空的会被反向绞杀

《2026年北美国别市场观察报告》

2025年美加墨（USMCA）三国贸易总额约1.59万亿美元，贸易节奏深度协同。墨西哥已取代加拿大及中国，成为美国第一大贸易伙伴。美国能源供给与制造业高度依赖邻国，加拿大提供美炼油厂23%的原油摄入量，墨西哥则承接了美国近一半的机动车零配件出口。宏观层面，美国通胀2.7%、失业率4.4%，加拿大通胀2.2%、失业率6.8%。AI基础设施投资驱动明显，罗素3000相关企业营收预期较2023年3月累计增加3000亿美元，AI投资占GDP峰值预期为2%。约49%的美国公司已将IT与软件开发近岸外包至加墨地区，北美近岸远程人才规模快速增长。

政策架构呈现“美国优先制度化”与“加拿大对冲性补贴”的双轨特征。美国通过《芯片与科学法案》（专项527亿美元）、《通胀削减法案》及《大而美法案》构建半导体与清洁能源补贴屏障，且CFIUS审查已扩围至AI、量子及能源等敏感领域，并针对特定对手（含中国）实施严厉准入限制。加拿大则通过战略响应基金（SRF）、战略创新基金（SIF）及加拿大增长基金（CGF）应对关税冲击，提供高比例成本报销，以撬动低碳技术与先进制造投入。美墨定于2026年5月启动USMCA首轮自动审议谈判，关键争议涉及原产地规则及关键矿产供应链安全。

产业布局上，中国企业倾向采用“中国研发部件+墨西哥组装+北美销售”的三角模式，选址向墨西哥北部蒙特雷集聚。信息技术市场预计年复合增长率（CAGR）为7.81%，智能制造市场规模预计2030年达857.2亿美元。金融科技领域预计CAGR为13.9%。中国企业在墨产能布局（如海信产能1000万台）虽享受零关税，但受EAR管制及337调查风险影响。未来USMCA审议可能收紧原产地规则，将直接威胁现有供应链合规性，企业需在谈判前完成原产地链路审计与合规风险对冲。

【关键词】：#USMCA #近岸外包 #原产地规则 #CFIUS #芯片与科学法案 #IRA #大而美法案 #SRF #SIF #CGF #美墨加谈判 #AI投资 #劳动生产率 #罗素3000 #蒙特雷 #海辰储能 #海信墨西哥 #美的北美 #智能制造 #布伦特油价 #EIA预测 #金融科技 #CETA #CPTPP #供应链审计 #合规风险 #EAR管制 #337调查 #近岸外包成本 #北美出海

【观点】：

报告属于典型的信息汇编型研究，数据源涵盖WTO、美国普查局、高盛及德勤等，可信度中等偏上。然而，必须区分“事实引用”与“投行假设”。高盛关于“生成式AI十年内提升美国劳动生产率15%且GDP增量4.5万亿美元”的预测属于情景假设，并非已实现事实，若投资者直接将其作为决策依据，面临极高的模型误差风险。

三个核心变量决定了北美出海的成败：一是USMCA 2026年审议的潜在不确定性，原产地规则收紧将直接击穿中国企业在墨组装的“免税合规”逻辑，该风险是当前最大的单点失效点，报告虽提及但权重过低。二是AI资本支出（Capex）的传导节奏，高盛预判的“峰值—回落”曲线意味着算力基建需求可能面临降速，加墨地区作为外包承接地，将经历从算力配套向软件应用侧的转型，企业需提前布局业务迁移路径。三是美欧关税僵局的外溢效应，美国对欧汽车加征25%关税若落地，势必导致欧洲车企竞相在北美（尤其是墨西哥）构建产能，从而挤占中国企业现有的集群空间与资源，这是报告未充分探讨的二阶影响。

逻辑盲点显著：报告将“近岸外包成本比美国低80%”泛化至加墨市场，这存在严重的统计口径误导。80%的成本优势仅适用于中美洲或加勒比海更低端的离岸人力，墨西哥软开成本实际仅比美国低约35%-50%。此外，报告未提示CFIUS对“穿透审查”的风险，即当中国背景企业在墨工厂的中方持股比例超过美方下游客户的合规阈值时，即便产品合规，依然会因下游客户的主动避险而被剔除出供应链。

抽象层级需提升至“供应链地缘政治”高度：北美出海已从简单的“制造外迁”演变为“合规身份管理”。企业不再仅仅是卖产品，而是在美墨边境通过资本合规与资产所有权穿透，管理一种“身份资产”。决策含义在于：企业必须将“地缘政治条款”写入商业合同，特别是针对原产地规则突变的成本分摊与解约机制。对能源侧而言，EIA预测的布伦特原油均价下行周期，隐含全球油市过剩假设，若OPEC+政策博弈超预期，原油价格波动将直接反噬墨西哥清洁能源项目的投资回报率。企业需在战略规划中引入敏感性测试，而非直接采纳投行基准预测。
